Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft}}) | 8 hours ({{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至|lt=Winter Solstice|lt_ja=冬至|qq=draft}}) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft}} | 10 2/3 hours | {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレット|lt=29. Peret I|lt_ja=29. ペレット I|qq=draft}} | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Mut (Egyptian Mythology)|ja|ムト|lt=Mut|lt_ja=ムト|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Chonsu (month)|ja|コンス|de|Chonsu (Monat)|lt=Chonsu|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft}} | 16 hours ({{ill|Summer Solstice|ja|夏至|lt=Summer Solstice|lt_ja=夏至|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Calendar (Antiquity)]] [[Category:Astronomical Data Collection]] [[Category:Historical Work (Astronomy)]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]]

{{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous {{ill|Ancient Greek|ja|古代ギリシア語|lt=Greek|lt_ja=ギリシア語|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to {{ill|Cairo|ja|カイロ|qq=draft}}, while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to {{ill|University of Oxford|ja|オックスフォード大学|lt=Oxford|lt_ja=オックスフォード|qq=draft}} and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|University of California|ja|カリフォルニア大学|qq=draft}}; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to {{ill|Copenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン|qq=draft}}. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by {{ill|Alan Gardiner|ja|アラン・ガーディナー|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Ja import failed]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous {{ill|Ancient Greek|ja|古代ギリシア語|lt=Greek|lt_ja=ギリシア語|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to {{ill|Cairo|ja|カイロ|qq=draft}}, while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to {{ill|University of Oxford|ja|オックスフォード大学|lt=Oxford|lt_ja=オックスフォード|qq=draft}} and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|University of California|ja|カリフォルニア大学|qq=draft}}; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to {{ill|Copenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン|qq=draft}}. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by {{ill|Alan Gardiner|ja|アラン・ガーディナー|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|lt=Gregorian Calendar|lt_ja=グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft}}. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft}}) | 8 hours ({{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至|lt=Winter Solstice|lt_ja=冬至|qq=draft}}) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft}} | 10 2/3 hours | {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレット|lt=29. Peret I|lt_ja=29. ペレット I|qq=draft}} | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Mut (Egyptian Mythology)|ja|ムト|lt=Mut|lt_ja=ムト|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Chonsu (month)|ja|コンス|de|Chonsu (Monat)|lt=Chonsu|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft}} | 16 hours ({{ill|Summer Solstice|ja|夏至|lt=Summer Solstice|lt_ja=夏至|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Calendar (Antiquity)]] [[Category:Astronomical Data Collection]] [[Category:Historical Work (Astronomy)]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]]

Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Calendar (Antiquity)]] [[Category:Astronomical Data Collection]] [[Category:Historical Work (Astronomy)]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

revision.comment: 33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender up to rev 217754523
revision.text:
{{Positionskarte+ |Ägypten |caption=Karte von Ägypten |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Ägypten |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} Der '''Tebtunis-Mondkalender''' wurde am 10. März 1931 unter der Leitung von [[Carlo Anti]] in [[Tebtunis]] entdeckt. Im Ort liegt der Tempel des Soknebtynis („Sobek, Herr von Tebtynis“) am südlichen Rand der Siedlung. Vor dem [[Naos (Architektur)|Naos]] liegt ein kleiner Hof. Die Anlage ist von einer [[Umfassungsmauer (Altägypten)|Umfassungsmauer]] umgeben, die auch weitere Gebäude, darunter einen kleinen Nebentempel und zahlreiche Priesterzellen einschließt. == Hintergrund == Die ersten Ausgrabungen an diesem Ort wurden 1899/1900 durch die beiden englischen Papyrologen B. P. Grenfell und A. S. Hunt durchgeführt. Sie legten Teile des Soknebtynis-Tempels und der umliegenden Siedlung frei, wobei sie zahlreiche [[Altgriechisch|griechische]] und [[Demotische Sprache|demotische]] [[Papyrologie|Papyri]] – das eigentliche Ziel ihrer Grabungen – fanden. Bei der Fundaufteilung kamen die meisten demotischen Papyri nach [[Kairo]], die griechischen und die noch in den Kartonagen steckenden dagegen zunächst zur Bearbeitung nach [[University of Oxford|Oxford]] und später – nach dem Tod von Grenfell und Hunt – in die [[Bancroft Library]] der [[University of California]]; die letzten zuvor übersehenen Kisten sind erst vor kurzem dort eingetroffen. Mit der Auswertung dieser Tebtunis-Papyri befasst sich das 2000 gegründete [[Center for the Tebtunis Papyri]] (CTP). Nach Kopenhagen gelangten dagegen vor allem literarische Texte in demotischer wie hieratischer Schrift. Sie stammen sicher aus der Bibliothek des Soknebtynis-Tempels. == Der Tebtunis-Mondkalender == [[Bild:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Jahreszeitenschema, Übersicht über die Referenzlinien der Erdbahn]] Der Bau der Tempelanlage wird auf das zweite Jahrhundert v. Chr. geschätzt. In Übereinstimmung dazu datiert der Tebtunis-Kalender, der detaillierte Angaben zu zahlreichen Festen sowie Angaben hinsichtlich der [[Stunden der Nacht]] und der [[Stunden des Tages]] enthält. Die von [[Alan Gardiner]] und [[Richard Anthony Parker]] geäußerte Vermutung, dass der [[Ägyptischer Kalender|ägyptische Kalender]] spätestens seit Anfang des [[Neues Reich|Neuen Reiches]] die Jahresform wechselte, findet im Kalender von Tebtunis eine weitere Bestätigung. Auffallend ist die Verwendung der alten Monatsnamen des [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ägyptischen Mondkalenders]], während im Hauptkalender die neu eingeführten Monatsnamen benutzt wurden. Zwei Mondmonatsnamen sind im Teptunis-Mondkalender anderen Gottheiten gewidmet. Weiter erfolgte ein Vorrücken des Monats [[Ka-her-ka]] auf die Position von [[Menchet (Monat)|Menchet]]. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''Der Tebtunis-Mondkalender im Vergleich zum ägyptischen Kalender um 132 v. Chr.''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !Tebtunis-Mondkalender !Tageslänge
(Buch der Stunden des Tages) !Ägyptischer Kalender !DatumVgl. Jean Meeus ''Astronomische Algorithmen - Anwendungen für Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Berechnungsprogramm Ephemeris Tool 4,5''.Daten des [[Gregorianischer Kalender|gregorianischen Kalenders]]. Zur Ermittlung der Referenzangaben im [[proleptisch]]en Kalender sind zwei Tage zu addieren. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |1. [[Techi]] |12 Stunden ([[Äquinoktium#Tagundnachtgleichen|Tagundnachtgleiche]]) |[[Achet|1. Achet I]] |23. September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |1. [[Ka-her-ka]] (statt Menchet) |10 2/3 Stunden |1. Achet II |23. Oktober |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |1. [[Hut-heru (Monat)|Hut-heru]] |9 1/3 Stunden |30. Achet II |21. November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |1. [[Sachmet]] (statt Ka-her-ka) |8 Stunden ([[Sonnenwende#Datum|Wintersonnenwende]]) |30. Achet III |21. Dezember |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |1. [[Schef-bedet]] |9 1/3 Stunden |29. Achet IV |19. Januar |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |1. [[Rekeh-wer]] |10 2/3 Stunden |[[Peret (Altes Ägypten)|29. Peret I]] |18. Februar |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |1. [[Rekeh-nedjes]] |12 Stunden (Tagundnachtgleiche) |28. Peret II |19. März |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |1. [[Mut (ägyptische Mythologie)|Mut]] (statt [[Renutet]]) |13 1/3 Stunden |28. Peret III |18. April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |1. [[Chonsu (Monat)|Chonsu]] |14 2/3 Stunden |27. Peret IV |17. Mai |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |1. [[Chenti-chet]] |16 Stunden (Sommersonnenwende) |[[Schemu|27. Schemu I]] |16. Juni |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |1. [[Ipet-hemet (Monat)|Ipet-hemet]] |14 2/3 Stunden |26. Schemu II |15. Juli |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |1. [[Wepet-renpet]] |13 1/3 Stunden |26. Schemu III |14. August |- |} == Siehe auch == * [[Ebers-Kalender]] == Literatur == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * [[Jean Meeus]]: ''Astronomische Algorithmen. Anwendungen für Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2. durchgesehene Auflage. Barth, Leipzig u. a. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Berechnungsprogramm Ephemeris Tool 4,5. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratische Papyri aus Tebtunis - Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * [[Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Band 107, 1994, S. 4–7. == Anmerkungen == == Einzelnachweise == [[Kategorie:Ägyptischer Kalender (Altertum)]] [[Kategorie:Astronomische Datensammlung]] [[Kategorie:Historisches Werk (Astronomie)]]

Chr. geschätzt. Die von [[Alan Gardiner]] und [[Richard Anthony Parker]] geäußerte Vermutung, dass der [[Ägyptischer Kalender|ägyptische Kalender]] spätestens seit Anfang des [[Neues Reich|Neuen Reiches]] die Jahresform wechselte, findet im Kalender von Tebtunis eine weitere Bestätigung. Auffallend ist die Verwendung der alten Monatsnamen des [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ägyptischen Mondkalenders]], während im Hauptkalender die neu eingeführten Monatsnamen benutzt wurden. Zwei Mondmonatsnamen sind im Teptunis-Mondkalender anderen Gottheiten gewidmet. Weiter erfolgte ein Vorrücken des Monats [[Ka-her-ka]] auf die Position von [[Menchet (Monat)|Menchet]]. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''Der Tebtunis-Mondkalender im Vergleich zum ägyptischen Kalender um 132 v. {{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous {{ill|Ancient Greek|ja|古代ギリシア語|lt=Greek|lt_ja=ギリシア語|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to {{ill|Cairo|ja|カイロ|qq=draft}}, while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to {{ill|University of Oxford|ja|オックスフォード大学|lt=Oxford|lt_ja=オックスフォード|qq=draft}} and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|University of California|ja|カリフォルニア大学|qq=draft}}; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to {{ill|Copenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン|qq=draft}}. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by {{ill|Alan Gardiner|ja|アラン・ガーディナー|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|lt=Gregorian Calendar|lt_ja=グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft}}. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft}}) | 8 hours ({{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至|lt=Winter Solstice|lt_ja=冬至|qq=draft}}) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft}} | 10 2/3 hours | {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレット|lt=29. Peret I|lt_ja=29. ペレット I|qq=draft}} | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Mut (Egyptian Mythology)|ja|ムト|lt=Mut|lt_ja=ムト|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Chonsu (month)|ja|コンス|de|Chonsu (Monat)|lt=Chonsu|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft}} | 16 hours ({{ill|Summer Solstice|ja|夏至|lt=Summer Solstice|lt_ja=夏至|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Calendar (Antiquity)]] [[Category:Astronomical Data Collection]] [[Category:Historical Work (Astronomy)]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] {{translated page|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|version=217754523|comment=Imported full de history}}

{{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous {{ill|Ancient Greek|ja|古代ギリシア語|lt=Greek|lt_ja=ギリシア語|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to {{ill|Cairo|ja|カイロ|qq=draft}}, while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to {{ill|University of Oxford|ja|オックスフォード大学|lt=Oxford|lt_ja=オックスフォード|qq=draft}} and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|University of California|ja|カリフォルニア大学|qq=draft}}; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to {{ill|Copenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン|qq=draft}}. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by {{ill|Alan Gardiner|ja|アラン・ガーディナー|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|lt=Gregorian Calendar|lt_ja=グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft}}. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft}}) | 8 hours ({{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至|lt=Winter Solstice|lt_ja=冬至|qq=draft}}) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft}} | 10 2/3 hours | {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレット|lt=29. Peret I|lt_ja=29. ペレット I|qq=draft}} | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Mut (Egyptian Mythology)|ja|ムト|lt=Mut|lt_ja=ムト|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Chonsu (month)|ja|コンス|de|Chonsu (Monat)|lt=Chonsu|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft}} | 16 hours ({{ill|Summer Solstice|ja|夏至|lt=Summer Solstice|lt_ja=夏至|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Calendar (Antiquity)]] [[Category:Astronomical Data Collection]] [[Category:Historical Work (Astronomy)]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|version=217754523|comment=Imported full de history}}

They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by {{ill|Alan Gardiner|ja|アラン・ガーディナー|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|lt=Gregorian Calendar|lt_ja=グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft}}. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft}}) | 8 hours ({{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至|lt=Winter Solstice|lt_ja=冬至|qq=draft}}) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft}} | 10 2/3 hours | {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレット|lt=29. Peret I|lt_ja=29. ペレット I|qq=draft}} | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Mut (Egyptian Mythology)|ja|ムト|lt=Mut|lt_ja=ムト|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Chonsu (month)|ja|コンス|de|Chonsu (Monat)|lt=Chonsu|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft}} | 16 hours ({{ill|Summer Solstice|ja|夏至|lt=Summer Solstice|lt_ja=夏至|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Calendar (Antiquity)]] [[Category:Astronomical Data Collection]] [[Category:Historical Work (Astronomy)]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|version=217754523|comment=Imported full de history}}

{{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous {{ill|Ancient Greek|ja|古代ギリシア語|lt=Greek|lt_ja=ギリシア語|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to {{ill|Cairo|ja|カイロ|qq=draft}}, while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to {{ill|University of Oxford|ja|オックスフォード大学|lt=Oxford|lt_ja=オックスフォード|qq=draft}} and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|University of California|ja|カリフォルニア大学|qq=draft}}; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to {{ill|Copenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン|qq=draft}}. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by {{ill|Alan Gardiner|ja|アラン・ガーディナー|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|lt=Gregorian Calendar|lt_ja=グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft}}. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft}}) | 8 hours ({{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至|lt=Winter Solstice|lt_ja=冬至|qq=draft}}) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft}} | 10 2/3 hours | {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレット|lt=29. Peret I|lt_ja=29. ペレット I|qq=draft}} | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Mut (Egyptian Mythology)|ja|ムト|lt=Mut|lt_ja=ムト|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Chonsu (month)|ja|コンス|de|Chonsu (Monat)|lt=Chonsu|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft}} | 16 hours ({{ill|Summer Solstice|ja|夏至|lt=Summer Solstice|lt_ja=夏至|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Calendar (Antiquity)]] [[Category:Astronomical Data Collection]] [[Category:Historical Work (Astronomy)]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages without German translation box]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|version=217754523|comment=Imported full de history}}

revision.text:
{{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous {{ill|Ancient Greek|ja|古代ギリシア語|lt=Greek|lt_ja=ギリシア語|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to {{ill|Cairo|ja|カイロ|qq=draft}}, while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to {{ill|University of Oxford|ja|オックスフォード大学|lt=Oxford|lt_ja=オックスフォード|qq=draft}} and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|University of California|ja|カリフォルニア大学|qq=draft}}; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to {{ill|Copenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン|qq=draft}}. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by {{ill|Alan Gardiner|ja|アラン・ガーディナー|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|lt=Gregorian Calendar|lt_ja=グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft}}. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft}}) | 8 hours ({{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至|lt=Winter Solstice|lt_ja=冬至|qq=draft}}) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft}} | 10 2/3 hours | {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレット|lt=29. Peret I|lt_ja=29. ペレット I|qq=draft}} | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Mut (Egyptian Mythology)|ja|ムト|lt=Mut|lt_ja=ムト|qq=draft}} (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Chonsu (month)|ja|コンス|de|Chonsu (Monat)|lt=Chonsu|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft}} | 16 hours ({{ill|Summer Solstice|ja|夏至|lt=Summer Solstice|lt_ja=夏至|qq=draft}}) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Calendar (Antiquity)]] [[Category:Astronomical Data Collection]] [[Category:Historical Work (Astronomy)]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|version=217754523|comment=Imported full de history}}

revision.text:
{{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carlo Anti|qq=}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis|qq=}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naos (Architecture)|qq=}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous {{ill|Ancient Greek|ja|古代ギリシア語|lt=Greek|lt_ja=ギリシア語|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Greek|qq=}} and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demotic language|qq=}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrology|qq=}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to {{ill|Cairo|ja|カイロ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairo|qq=}}, while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to {{ill|University of Oxford|ja|オックスフォード大学|lt=Oxford|lt_ja=オックスフォード|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Oxford|qq=}} and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bancroft Library|qq=}} of the {{ill|University of California|ja|カリフォルニア大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of California|qq=}}; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|qq=}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to {{ill|Copenhagen|ja|コペンハーゲン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Copenhagen|qq=}}. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by {{ill|Alan Gardiner|ja|アラン・ガーディナー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alan Gardiner|qq=}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koiak|qq=}} was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|lt=Gregorian Calendar|lt_ja=グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}}. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/proleptic calendar|qq=}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet|qq=}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Koiak|ja|カー・ヘルカ|de|Ka-her-ka|lt=Ka-her-ka|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koiak|qq=}} (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hathor (month)|qq=}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekhmet|qq=}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka-her-ka|qq=}}) | 8 hours ({{ill|Winter Solstice|ja|冬至|lt=Winter Solstice|lt_ja=冬至|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Solstice|qq=}}) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schef-bedet|qq=}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-wer|qq=}} | 10 2/3 hours | {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレット|lt=29. Peret I|lt_ja=29. ペレット I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-nedjes|qq=}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Mut (Egyptian Mythology)|ja|ムト|lt=Mut|lt_ja=ムト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mut (Egyptian Mythology)|qq=}} (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renutet|qq=}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Chonsu (month)|ja|コンス|de|Chonsu (Monat)|lt=Chonsu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chonsu (month)|qq=}} | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khenti-khet|qq=}} | 16 hours ({{ill|Summer Solstice|ja|夏至|lt=Summer Solstice|lt_ja=夏至|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Solstice|qq=}}) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipet-hemet (month)|qq=}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wepet-renpet|qq=}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{translated page|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|version=217754523|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Calendar (Antiquity)]] [[Category:Astronomical Data Collection]] [[Category:Historical Work (Astronomy)]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]]

Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.''
