Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carlo Anti|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naos (Architecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous [[:en:Ancient Greek|Greek]] and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demotic language|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to [[:en:Cairo|Cairo]], while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to [[:en:University of Oxford|Oxford]] and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bancroft Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:University of California|University of California]]; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to [[:en:Copenhagen|Copenhagen]]. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian Calendar]]. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/proleptic calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hathor (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekhmet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka-her-ka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 8 hours ([[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]]) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schef-bedet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-wer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 10 2/3 hours | [[:en:Pellet|29. Peret I]] | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-nedjes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. [[:en:Mut|Mut]] (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renutet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. [[:en:Khonsu|Chonsu]] | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khenti-khet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 16 hours ([[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]]) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipet-hemet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wepet-renpet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{translated page|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|version=217754523|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Astronomical catalogues]] [[Category:Historical work in Astronomy]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{translated page|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|version=217754523|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Astronomical catalogues]] [[Category:Historical work in Astronomy]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1458800]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carlo Anti|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naos (Architecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous [[:en:Ancient Greek|Greek]] and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demotic language|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to [[:en:Cairo|Cairo]], while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to [[:en:University of Oxford|Oxford]] and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bancroft Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:University of California|University of California]]; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to [[:en:Copenhagen|Copenhagen]]. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian Calendar]]. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/proleptic calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hathor (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekhmet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka-her-ka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 8 hours ([[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]]) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schef-bedet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-wer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 10 2/3 hours | [[:en:Pellet|29. Peret I]] | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-nedjes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. [[:en:Mut|Mut]] (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renutet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. [[:en:Khonsu|Chonsu]] | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khenti-khet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 16 hours ([[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]]) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipet-hemet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wepet-renpet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{translated page|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|version=217754523|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Astronomical catalogues]] [[Category:Historical work in Astronomy]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1458800]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carlo Anti|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naos (Architecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous [[:en:Ancient Greek|Greek]] and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demotic language|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to [[:en:Cairo|Cairo]], while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to [[:en:University of Oxford|Oxford]] and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bancroft Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:University of California|University of California]]; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to [[:en:Copenhagen|Copenhagen]]. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian Calendar]]. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/proleptic calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. revision.comment: Text replacement - "Wikidata entry [[d:" to "Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q1458800]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carlo Anti|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naos (Architecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous [[:en:Ancient Greek|Greek]] and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demotic language|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to [[:en:Cairo|Cairo]], while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to [[:en:University of Oxford|Oxford]] and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bancroft Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:University of California|University of California]]; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to [[:en:Copenhagen|Copenhagen]]. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法|1=rekihou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514955}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian Calendar]]. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/proleptic calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hathor (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekhmet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka-her-ka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 8 hours ([[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]]) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schef-bedet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-wer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 10 2/3 hours | [[:en:Pellet|29. Peret I]] | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-nedjes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. [[:en:Mut|Mut]] (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renutet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. [[:en:Khonsu|Chonsu]] | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khenti-khet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 16 hours ([[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]]) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipet-hemet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. Schemu II | 15 July |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペット・レンペット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wepet-renpet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 13 1/3 hours | 26. Schemu III | 14 August |} == See Also == * {{ill|Ebers calendar|ja|エバーズ暦|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Data: Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Algorithms. Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' 2nd revised edition. Barth, Leipzig et al. 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program. * Jürgen Osing: ''Hieratic Papyri from Tebtunis – Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Near Eastern Studies.'' Museum Tusculanum Press, University of Copenhagen 1998, ISBN 87-7289-280-3. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Arthur M.F.W. Verhoogt: ''The Tebtunis Papyri at The Bancroft Library.'' In: ''Bancroftiana.'' Volume 107, 1994, pp. 4–7. == Notes == == Citations == {{translated page|de|Tebtunis-Mondkalender|version=217754523|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Tebtunis lunar calendar]] [[de:Tebtunis-Mondkalender]] [[ja:テブトゥニス月暦]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Astronomical catalogues]] [[Category:Historical work in Astronomy]] [[Category:Egyptian calendar]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tebtunis lunar calendar]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q1458800]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carlo Anti|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naos (Architecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous [[:en:Ancient Greek|Greek]] and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demotic language|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to [[:en:Cairo|Cairo]], while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to [[:en:University of Oxford|Oxford]] and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bancroft Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:University of California|University of California]]; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to [[:en:Copenhagen|Copenhagen]]. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法|1=rekihou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514955}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! (Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法|1=rekihou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514955}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian Calendar]]. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/proleptic calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q1458800]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carlo Anti|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naos (Architecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous [[:en:Ancient Greek|Greek]] and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demotic language|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to [[:en:Cairo|Cairo]], while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to [[:en:University of Oxford|Oxford]] and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bancroft Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:University of California|University of California]]; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to [[:en:Copenhagen|Copenhagen]]. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法|1=rekihou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514955}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian Calendar]]. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] (instead of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 10 2/3 hours | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hathor (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-wer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 10 2/3 hours | [[:en:Pellet|29. Peret I]] | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-nedjes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. [[:en:Mut|Mut]] (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renutet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous [[:en:Ancient Greek|Greek]] and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demotic language|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to [[:en:Cairo|Cairo]], while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to [[:en:University of Oxford|Oxford]] and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bancroft Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:University of California|University of California]]; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to [[:en:Copenhagen|Copenhagen]]. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法|1=rekihou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514955}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! DateCf. {{ill|Jean Meeus|ja|ジャン・ミューズ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jean Meeus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''Astronomical Algorithms – Applications for Ephemeris Tool 4,5.'' Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian Calendar]]. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/proleptic calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1. Achet I|lt_ja=1. アシェト I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 23 September |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. Achet II | 23 October |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Hathor (month)|ja|フト・ヘル|Hathor (month)=Hathor (month)|lt=Hut-heru|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hathor (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 9 1/3 hours | 30. Achet II | 21 November |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Sekhmet|ja|サクメット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekhmet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (instead of {{ill|Ka-her-ka|ja|カー・ヘルカ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ka-her-ka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 8 hours ([[:en:Winter solstice|Winter Solstice]]) | 30. Achet III | 21 December |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Schef-bedet|ja|シェフ・ベデット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schef-bedet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 9 1/3 hours | 29. Achet IV | 19 January |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-wer|ja|レケ・ウェル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-wer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 10 2/3 hours | [[:en:Pellet|29. Peret I]] | 18 February |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Rekeh-nedjes|ja|レケ・ネジェス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekeh-nedjes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 28. Peret II | 19 March |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. [[:en:Mut|Mut]] (instead of {{ill|Renutet|ja|レヌテット|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renutet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | 13 1/3 hours | 28. Peret III | 18 April |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. [[:en:Khonsu|Chonsu]] | 14 2/3 hours | 27. Peret IV | 17 May |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | 1. {{ill|Khenti-khet|ja|チェンティ・チェット|de|Chenti-chet|Khenti-khet=Khenti-khet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Khenti-khet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 16 hours ([[:en:Summer solstice|Summer Solstice]]) | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|ja|シェムウ|lt=27. Schemu I|lt_ja=27. シェムウ I|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 16 June |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Ipet-hemet (month)|ja|イペット・ヘメット|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|lt=Ipet-hemet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ipet-hemet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 14 2/3 hours | 26. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q1458800]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carlo Anti|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naos (Architecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous [[:en:Ancient Greek|Greek]] and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demotic language|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to [[:en:Cairo|Cairo]], while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to [[:en:University of Oxford|Oxford]] and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bancroft Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:University of California|University of California]]; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to [[:en:Copenhagen|Copenhagen]]. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法|1=rekihou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514955}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q1458800]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Positionskarte+ |Egypt |caption=Map of Egypt |width=200 |float=right |places= {{Positionskarte~ |Egypt |label= '''Tebtunis''' |lat = 29/7/0/N |long = 30/45/0/E |region=EG-FYM |position=right }} }} The '''Tebtunis Lunar Calendar''' was discovered on March 10, 1931, under the direction of {{ill|Carlo Anti|ja|カルロ・アンティ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carlo Anti|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Tebtunis|ja|テブトゥニス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tebtunis|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the town, the temple of Soknebtynis (“Sobek, Lord of Tebtynis”) is located on the southern edge of the settlement. In front of the {{ill|Naos (Architecture)|ja|ナオス (建築)|lt=Naos|lt_ja=ナオス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naos (Architecture)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lies a small courtyard. The complex is surrounded by an {{ill|Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|ja|囲いの壁 (古代エジプト)|lt=Enclosure Wall|lt_ja=囲いの壁|de|Umfassungsmauer_(Altägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enclosure Wall (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also encloses other buildings, including a small subsidiary temple and numerous priest cells. == Background == The first excavations at this site were conducted in 1899/1900 by the two English papyrologists B. P. Grenfell and A. S. Hunt. They uncovered parts of the Soknebtynis temple and the surrounding settlement, discovering numerous [[:en:Ancient Greek|Greek]] and {{ill|Demotic language|ja|デモティック語|lt=Demotic|lt_ja=デモティック|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demotic language|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Papyrology|ja|パピロロジー|lt=Papyri|lt_ja=パピロロジー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the actual goal of their excavations. In the division of finds, most of the demotic papyri went to [[:en:Cairo|Cairo]], while the Greek ones and those still in the boxes were initially sent for processing to [[:en:University of Oxford|Oxford]] and later – after the death of Grenfell and Hunt – to the {{ill|Bancroft Library|ja|バンクロフト図書館|de|Bancroft Library|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bancroft Library|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:University of California|University of California]]; the last boxes that had been overlooked earlier have only recently arrived there. The analysis of these Tebtunis papyri is handled by the {{ill|Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|ja|テブトゥニス・パピルス研究センター|de|Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|de=Center_for_the_Tebtunis_Papyri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Center for the Tebtunis Papyri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, founded in 2000. In contrast, literary texts in demotic as well as hieratic script were sent to [[:en:Copenhagen|Copenhagen]]. They undoubtedly originate from the library of the Soknebtynis temple. == The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar == [[File:Jahreszeiten99 DE2.jpg|right|400px|Seasonal diagram, overview of the reference lines of the Earth’s orbit]] The construction of the temple complex is estimated to date from the second century BC. Accordingly, the Tebtunis calendar, which contains detailed information on numerous festivals as well as details regarding the {{ill|Hours of the Night|ja|夜の時間|lt=Hours of the Night|lt_ja=夜の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Night|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Hours of the Day|ja|日の時間|lt=Hours of the Day|lt_ja=日の時間|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hours of the Day|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is dated to that period. The hypothesis expressed by [[:en:Alan Gardiner|Alan Gardiner]] and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|ja|リチャード・アンソニー・パーカー|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that the {{ill|Egyptian Calendar|ja|エジプト暦|lt=Egyptian Calendar|lt_ja=エジプト暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法|1=rekihou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514955}} had changed its annual form at the latest since the beginning of the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is further confirmed by the Tebtunis calendar. Notably, the ancient month names of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=月暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are used, while in the main calendar the newly introduced month names were employed. Two lunar month names in the Tebtunis Lunar Calendar are dedicated to other deities. Furthermore, the month [[:en:Koiak|Ka-her-ka]] was advanced to the position of {{ill|Menchet (month)|ja|メンシェット|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=メンシェット|de|Menchet (Monat)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet (month)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |+ '''The Tebtunis Lunar Calendar in Comparison to the Egyptian Calendar around 132 BC''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! Tebtunis Lunar Calendar ! Length of Day
(Book of the Hours of the Day) ! Egyptian Calendar ! Barth, Leipzig 2000, ISBN 3-335-00400-0, ''Ephemeris Tool 4,5 Calculation Program''.Data of the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian Calendar]]. To determine the reference values in the {{ill|proleptic calendar|ja|先取暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/proleptic calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} two days must be added. |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | 1. {{ill|Techi|ja|テヒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | 12 hours ({{ill|Equinox|ja|春分・秋分|lt=Equinox|lt_ja=春分・秋分|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Equinox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | {{ill|Achet|ja|アシェト|lt=1.
